page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server instance configured with an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 12 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 24 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory performance is robust, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 25%, while write bandwidth and small-block cached read bandwidth remain in the average tier. For machine learning tasks, the hardware achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 345.71 tokens per second during small LLM inference, though prompt processing speeds align with average tiers.

The qualitative cost profile of this server is defined by its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and the integration of a mid-range NVIDIA L4 GPU, offering a resource-dense configuration without the overhead of dedicated physical CPU cores. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential performance tradeoff under sustained, multi-core CPU-bound workloads, though single-core execution remains highly competitive. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs and performance will depend on attached network volumes. This configuration is structurally aligned with workloads such as small-scale LLM inference, machine learning model evaluation, GPU-accelerated data processing, and memory-intensive analytical tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor with a shared CPU allocation, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng and compression benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. Large-block memory read bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU and memory write metrics remain average. The instance lacks local storage, requiring network-attached volumes. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, machine learning inference, and GPU-accelerated data processing where dedicated CPU cores are not a strict requirement.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM, designed for machine learning and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL4 features 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A memory-dense, GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU and AMD EPYC processor for machine learning inference and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB VRAM for accelerated computing.
12 vCPUs on AMD EPYC 9575F architecture with shared allocation.
128.0 GB of system memory providing 10.67 GB per core.
Top-tier single-core CPU and text-generation inference performance.
No local storage included, requiring network-attached volumes.
Suited for machine learning inference, data science, and analytical tasks.
